items.subject: Your Together Loans Loan : ****
items.body: Dear Together Loans, Thank you for your recent response regarding my request to be removed as guarantor on Loan Number **** for borrower **** ****. I understand your explanation of the dual‑liability structure and that, under standard circumstances, you do not release a co‑signer once the loan is in place. However, I am asking that you reconsider in light of information that goes directly to how my consent was obtained and whether the screening process you required was properly followed. Approximately one week before I agreed to co‑sign, I was the victim in a domestic‑violence incident in which my father physically attacked me. At the time of the loan process, I was still in a highly vulnerable emotional and psychological state from that event. **** was fully aware of this vulnerability and of the trauma I had just experienced. In addition, **** currently has active criminal charges in Florida involving domestic violence and multiple felonies (including theft of a firearm, property damage, and burglary). Knowing both his history and my then‑recent assault, I felt immense pressure and fear around the time I agreed to co‑sign. I did not feel that I was in the proper state of mind or

items.subject: Your Together Loans Loan : ****
items.body: Good Evening, I believe I already mentioned in one of my Voice Messages to U back when I was trying to reach **** to let him know that the payment date had changed with the new loan. His phone wasn't taking voice messages for "minute", but I DID text him his new loan payment was NOT due on the 8th of each month but rather on the 3rd of each month. He then texted me back that his CHIME Bank Account &/or Debit Card had been "compromised", a fraudulent charge was on there, so they recommended closing that account/debit card & sending another one out in the mail which would probably take about 7 to 10 business days to receive. Thus he asked me if I could please cover his payment at this time, but he should be back up & running for May's payment. Thank you for assisting us with this transition to a new loan & paying off the old one - this should boost his credit score as well. Sincerely, **** ****, Co Signer for **** ****

items.subject: Action Required: **** Bounced Loan Payout
items.body: I'll send over a void check shortly. Thanks! Sent a copy to through text message

items.subject: Action Required: **** Bounced Loan Payout
items.body: *Good Morning Berginie,* *Hope you are doing well this morning!* *Regarding the loan transfer - I called my bank yesterday to see if a pending transfer was showing in my account. They showed nothing.* *For this loan, I would like to change my bank and banking information. I am attaching a VOIDED check to confirm the routing and account number. This account is the same account for the debit card provided over the phone.* *Please let me know if you need any additional information.* *Thank you,* ***** **** *

items.subject: Action Required: **** Bounced Loan Payout
items.body: I have confirmed the account by attaching the statement and the routing number. ****
**** **** is the loan holder Birth Day 5/25/1960 Email **** Cosigner **** ****
